name: 5-Amino-1MQ
subtitle: NNMT Inhibitor | Longevity & Metabolic Enhancement
overview: 5-Amino-1MQ is a small molecule NNMT (nicotinamide N-methyltransferase) inhibitor that enhances cellular NAD+ levels and mitochondrial function. Not a true peptide, but a small molecule compound used in longevity and metabolic optimization protocols.
mechanism: Inhibits NNMT enzyme systemically, preventing NAD+ degradation and maintaining elevated cellular NAD+ levels to support mitochondrial function and metabolic processes.

items.name: NAD+ Enhancement
items.description: Significantly increases cellular NAD+ levels by inhibiting NNMT enzyme, supporting cellular energy production and DNA repair mechanisms essential for healthy aging.

items.name: Mitochondrial Function
items.description: Improves mitochondrial biogenesis and efficiency, enhancing cellular energy production and reducing oxidative stress markers associated with aging.

items.name: Cellular Repair
items.description: Enhanced NAD+ availability supports sirtuins and other longevity pathways involved in DNA repair, cellular maintenance, and stress resistance.

deliveryMethods.overview: Most common delivery method. Take with food to improve absorption and reduce potential GI effects. Consistent daily timing helps maintain steady NAD+ levels. Morning or early afternoon dosing preferred.
Injectable protocols exist but vary widely. CRITICAL: Two vastly different protocols exist differing by 100-200x depending on units (mcg vs mg). Verify units before use.

steps: CRITICAL: Confirm protocol (mcg vs mg) before proceeding
Inject bacteriostatic water slowly down side of vial
Label clearly with concentration AND units (mg/mL)

interactions.notes: May work synergistically by preventing breakdown while supporting production.
Both support longevity; resveratrol activates sirtuins while 5-Amino-1MQ provides NAD+ substrate.
Both affect metabolic pathways; combination may enhance effects but requires careful monitoring.
No known interactions; different mechanisms with potential complementary benefits.
Limited interaction data; consult healthcare provider before combining.
Both affect metabolic pathways; potential additive glucose metabolism effects.

timeline.effects: Gradual energy increase and mental clarity
Improved metabolic markers and body composition
Sustained longevity benefits and cellular health improvements

common: Potential sleep interference if dosed late in day
stopSigns: Severe gastrointestinal upset or persistent nausea
Unusual fatigue or weakness (opposite of expected effect)
monitoring: Start with lowest effective dose (25-50mg) to assess tolerance
Take with food to reduce potential GI effects
Monitor energy levels and sleep quality

good: White to off-white (or orange/amber for powder) appearance
Uniform color and size with clear labeling and batch information
Proper pharmaceutical packaging with moisture protection
Clear expiration dates and professional labeling
warning: Moisture damage or clumping may indicate degradation

references.title: Selective and membrane-permeable small molecule inhibitors of nicotinamide N-methyltransferase reverse high fat diet-induced obesity in mice
references.authors: Neelakantan H, Vance V, Wetzel MD, Wang HL, McHardy SF, Finnerty CC, Hommel JD, Watowich SJ
references.keyFindings: Diet-induced obese mice showed significant weight loss and adipocyte size reduction with 5-amino-1MQ treatment. Demonstrated that NNMT inhibition increases energy expenditure, reduces body weight and white adipose mass, improves insulin sensitivity, and normalizes glucose tolerance.

references.title: Development & validation of LC-MS/MS assay for 5-amino-1-methyl quinolinium in rat plasma: Application to pharmacokinetic and oral bioavailability studies
references.journal: Journal of Pharmaceutical and Biomedical Analysis
references.keyFindings: Demonstrated 38.4% oral bioavailability, half-life 6.9hr (oral) and 3.8hr (IV). Validated sensitive LC-MS/MS method for pharmacokinetic assessment supporting oral administration viability.

references.title: Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase inhibition mimics and boosts exercise-mediated improvements in muscle function in aged mice
references.keyFindings: NNMTi-treated aged sedentary mice showed ~40% greater grip strength than controls. Combined with exercise, 60% grip strength increase. Also improved intramuscular lipid content and gastrocnemius fiber cross-sectional area.

latestResearch.title: Nicotinamide N-methyltransferase inhibition mitigates obesity-related metabolic dysfunction
latestResearch.summary: Validated NNMT inhibition with 5A1MQ as a viable pharmacological approach to ameliorate metabolic imbalances and improve liver pathologies in diet-induced obese mice. 5A1MQ dose-dependently limited body weight and fat mass gain without altering food intake.

latestResearch.title: Identification of nicotinamide N-methyltransferase as a promising therapeutic target for sarcopenia
latestResearch.summary: Used machine learning to identify NNMT as a key metabolic dysregulation gene in sarcopenia. NNMT inhibition improved grip strength, alleviated age-related decline in muscle mass, and increased NAD+ content and PGC1α expression in aging mice.

quickStats.storage: Oral: room temp. Injectable: lyophilized room temp or freezer, reconstituted 2-8°C for 28 days
